name: EngFlow
description: Remote execution and caching platform for Bazel and other REAPI-compatible build tools. Founded by ex-Google engineers who built Googles internal build infrastructure.

seo.title: EngFlow — Monorepo & Build Systems
seo.meta_description: EngFlow: Remote execution and caching platform for Bazel and other REAPI-compatible build tools. Founded by ex-Google engineers who built Googles internal build

when_to_use: Remote Bazel execution on auto-scaling cloud workers
Build caching shared across engineers and CI agents
REAPI-compatible caching for non-Bazel build tools
Cut Bazel build times by distributing across machines

pros: Serious Bazel remote execution and caching performance
Founded by the team that built Google's Blaze infra
REAPI-compatible, works with Buck2 and others
cons: Priced and sold to large engineering orgs
Requires non-trivial Bazel expertise to benefit
Overkill unless builds are truly a bottleneck
review.verdict: Top-tier choice for serious Bazel shops; small teams will get little value versus the cost and complexity.
review.best_for: Large Bazel or Buck2 monorepos with painful build and test times
review.not_for: Teams not using Bazel or without dedicated build engineers

pricing.high_water_mark_reason: Custom/contact sales alone, no explicit peak-lock evidence
